folders.name: Programming Basics
folders.description: Fundamental concepts for coding interviews and exams

subjects.name: C Language
subjects.description: Syntax, pointers, memory, and control flow

cards.front: How do you declare an integer variable in C?
cards.back: Use int variableName; Example: int marks;
A pointer stores the memory address of another variable.
It lets a function modify original variables and avoids copying large data.

subjects.name: Data Structures
subjects.description: Arrays, linked lists, stacks, and trees

cards.back: It occurs when you push more items than the stack capacity allows.

cards.front: Identify this structure and one key property.
cards.back: Binary tree: each node has at most two children.

cards.front: State the power rule for differentiation.
cards.back: If f(x) = x^n, then f'(x) = n*x^(n-1).

cards.front: When do we use the chain rule?
cards.back: When a function is composed inside another function, e.g., f(g(x)).

cards.front: What is the range of probability values?
